title: austromyrtus lasioclada
intro: Austromyrtus lasioclada is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family, Myrtaceae, native to the eastern coast of Australia. It is also known as the 'Native Geraldton Waxflower' or 'Native Waxflower', and is prized for its beautiful, fragrant flowers and potential medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Austromyrtus lasioclada can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, flu, and skin conditions.
